mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS year-over-year growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation combined with a low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary environment, reducing cost pressures and borrowing costs for corporations, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts during a period of rising interest rates indicates a severe slowdown in construction and related industries, signaling broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% YoY during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising), but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating healthy supply-side capacity and productivity, which supports long-term corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is consistently positive (e.g., 3-month average > 0.1%) but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust job market and strong employment growth, leading to increased consumer confidence and spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 150k, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly rising inflation combined with an increasing federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening to combat overheating, which increases discount rates and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3.0% YoY and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 3 months
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth during a period of low unemployment indicates a mature economic expansion with high capacity utilization, leading to sustained corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% YoY while UNRATE is below 5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.
